$comment: Receipt canonical input + sha256 digest. The capability tag 'cloister/credential-isolation/v1' is pinned as the first field — domain-separation: a different capability re-using sha256+LF produces different bytes for the same field values, so receipts can't be cross-replayed between capabilities. All forbidden MUST-NOT-COMMIT fields (credential value, allowedSubs, request/response body, query, KEK source URL) are STRUCTURALLY absent — there is no key for them in the canonical input grammar.

vectors.name: openai_success_200
vectors.description: Successful proxied chat-completion. peer_fp is all-a fake; ts/nonce shared with proxy-envelope-canonical.json:happy_bearer_chat_completion for cross-referenceability. upstream_status=200, request/response sizes pinned.

vectors.name: github_404_upstream
vectors.description: Upstream returned 404. The receipt commits to upstream_status=404 — the proxy faithfully reports upstream's status. NOTE: this is the upstream's 404, not the proxy's own constant-time 404 (those don't generate receipts because no credential lookup happens for them).

vectors.name: oauth_token_400_bad_request
vectors.description: Upstream returned 400 (e.g. bad grant_type). Receipt still commits to the URL path and status even on error — observers see a peer's failed attempts, but the request body (which would contain client_id + secret in OAuth client_credentials shape) is NEVER part of the receipt input.
